task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Authenticated Todo API** (Level 5)

Build a Todo API that requires JWT authentication. POST /api/register creates a user (email, password). POST /api/login returns a token. All /api/todos endpoints require auth. Users can only see their own todos.

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build for javascript backend

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required

create a Todos table:
  title, required
  done
  user_id, required

# Your endpoints here

```
final_source:
/*
ARCHITECTURE:
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│     Authentication Todo API             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Tables:                                │
│  - Users (email, password)              │
│  - Todos (title, done, user_id)         │
│                                         │
│  Auth Endpoints:                        │
│  - POST /api/register → create user     │
│  - POST /api/login → return token       │
│                                         │
│  Todo Endpoints (require login):        │
│  - GET /api/todos → list user's todos   │
│  - POST /api/todos → create new todo    │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
*/

build for javascript backend

owner is 'test@example.com'
allow signup and login
database is local memory
log every request

create a Users table:
  email, required, unique
  password, required

create a Todos table:
  title, required
  done, default false
  user_id, required

rate limit 10 per minute

# =====================
# AUTHENTICATION ENDPOINTS
# =====================

when user sends credentials to /api/register:
  validate credentials:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required, min 6
  
  # Check if user already exists
  existing = look up User where email is credentials's email
  if existing is not nothing:
    send back 'Email already registered' with status 400
  
  # Save user
  new_user = save credentials as new User
  send back new_user with status 201

when user sends credentials to /api/login:
  validate credentials:
    email is text, required, matches email
    password is text, required
  
  # Find user by email
  user = look up User where email is credentials's email
  if user is nothing:
    send back 'Invalid email or password' with status 401
  
  # In this demo, we store password in plaintext and compare directly
  if user's password is not credentials's password:
    send back 'Invalid email or password' with status 401
  
  # Return success with token (the user's id)
  # Return success with token (the user's id)

# =====================
# TODO ENDPOINTS
# =====================

when user requests data from /api/todos:
  requires login
  
  # Get all todos for the authenticated user
  todos = get all Todos where user_id is caller's id
  send back todos

when user sends todo to /api/todos:
  requires login
  
  validate todo:
    title is text, required, min 1
  
  # Create todo for authenticated user
  create new_todo:
    title is todo's title
    user_id is caller's id
    done is false
  
  saved_todo = save new_todo as new Todo
  send back saved_todo with status 201

when user updates todo at /api/todos/:id:
  requires login
  
  # Get the todo and verify ownership
  todo = look up Todo with this id
  if todo is nothing:
    send back 'Todo not found' with status 404
  
  if todo's user_id is not caller's id:
    send back 'Unauthorized' with status 403
  
  # Update the todo
  todo's title is todo's title
  todo's done is todo's done
  
  save todo to Todos
  send back todo

when user deletes todo at /api/todos/:id:
  requires login
  
  # Get the todo and verify ownership
  todo = look up Todo with this id
  if todo is nothing:
    send back 'Todo not found' with status 404
  
  if todo's user_id is not caller's id:
    send back 'Unauthorized' with status 403
  
  # Delete the todo
  delete the Todo with this id
  send back 'Deleted' with status 204

# =====================
# TESTS
# =====================

test 'user can register':
  call POST /api/register with email is 'test@example.com' and password is 'secret123'
  expect response status is 201

test 'user can login':
  call POST /api/register with email is 'test2@example.com' and password is 'secret123'
  call POST /api/login with email is 'test2@example.com' and password is 'secret123'
  expect response status is 200
  expect response has 'token'

test 'get todos without auth returns 401':
  call GET /api/todos
  expect response status is 401

test 'authenticated user can create todo':
  call POST /api/register with email is 'test3@example.com' and password is 'secret123'
  call POST /api/login with email is 'test3@example.com' and password is 'secret123'
  call POST /api/todos with title is 'Buy groceries'
  expect response status is 201
  expect response has 'id'
  expect response has 'title'
